The 2026 FIFA World Cup takes place from June 11 to July 19, 2026. This market resolves YES if Norway wins the tournament. Resolution will be determined by the official FIFA World Cup 2026 final results, available at https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026.

Background

Norway qualified directly for the World Cup finals as group winners in UEFA qualifying. The 2026 FIFA World Cup expanded to 48 teams, with teams split into twelve groups of four teams, with the top two teams in each group and the eight best third-placed teams progressing to a new round of 32. The tournament will be jointly hosted by sixteen cities—eleven in the United States, three in Mexico, and two in Canada.

Considerations

Norway has not won a FIFA World Cup in its history. The tournament's expanded 48-team format means more teams advance from the group stage, potentially creating a more unpredictable knockout phase. Norway is drawn in Group I alongside France, Senegal, and a UEFA playoff winner.

This description was generated by AI.
reference_class: Eliminated teams in single-elimination knockout tournaments winning the tournament
reasoning: Already-resolved check (mandatory first step) found the decisive fact: Norway was eliminated from the 2026 World Cup, losing 2-1 to England in extra time in the quarterfinal on July 11, 2026 (Bellingham scored twice; Norway led via Schjelderup). This is corroborated by England FA's own match centre, CBS Sports, Al Jazeera, and ESPN/Yahoo coverage of the bracket. Norway topped Group I (beat Senegal 3-2, lost to France 1-4) and advanced through the round of 32/16, but the quarterfinal loss ends any path to winning the tournament — a team cannot win a single-elimination competition after elimination. Reference class: probability an eliminated knockout-tournament team wins the tournament = 0 by construction. Base rate before this evidence (pre-tournament reference class of a good-but-not-elite European nation with a star player) was already low (~2-4%); post-elimination it collapses to the residual floor for source/reporting error only.
what_would_change_my_mind: Credible evidence that the reported quarterfinal result is incorrect or that FIFA's official records show a different outcome
Any indication the match was later voided/replayed due to a formal protest with a different final outcome

question: Will Mitch McConnell make a verifiable, live public appearance before August 3, 2026?
resolution_criterion:
This market resolves YES if Mitch McConnell makes a live public appearance, or appears in clearly same-day public video, before August 3, 2026.

Deadline: August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

To count, the appearance must be independently verifiable through a source such as C-SPAN, a Senate livestream, live TV, a public Senate proceeding, or footage/reporting from a major news organization.

It does not count if the only evidence is released by McConnell’s campaign, Senate office, family, allies, or personal/social media accounts. 

Written statements, private calls, old footage, undated photos/videos, and claims that he is “working” or “recovering” without public visual evidence also do not count.

This market resolves NO if the deadline passes without a qualifying appearance.

—

Why August 3: Kentucky changed its U.S. Senate vacancy law in 2024. The governor can no longer appoint an interim senator; vacancies are filled by special election. Recent reporting suggests August 3 may be a key cutoff: a vacancy before then could trigger a separate special-election process, while a vacancy on or after then may effectively be folded into the regular November election. (The law is new and the exact process could be contested.)
reference_class: Elderly (80+) US officials hospitalized after a severe cardiac/neurological event (cardiac arrest w/ CPR, stroke), time to first independently-verifiable public appearance
reasoning: McConnell suffered an apparent cardiac arrest requiring CPR on June 14, 2026, and has been hospitalized continuously since with zero independently verifiable public appearance for 4+ weeks despite intense pressure (Beshear's public letter, death-hoax rumors, unconfirmed brain-death reports from independent journalists). Office statements are vague written releases that explicitly do not qualify under this market's criteria; third-hand claims of phone conversations (Barrasso, Scott Jennings) also don't qualify. Reference class: Fetterman's materially less severe 2022 ischemic stroke still took ~9 weeks to produce any public appearance; McConnell's cardiac-arrest event carries higher hypoxic brain injury risk and he's already 4 weeks in with only ~3 weeks left before the Aug 2 deadline. Countervailing: strong political incentive (Senate back in session, vacancy-law scrutiny, rumor control) could push his team toward a brief camera-verifiable appearance (e.g., a floor vote, which requires physical presence since the Senate has no proxy voting) if he is genuinely improving as claimed. Net: leans NO but not overwhelmingly.
what_would_change_my_mind: Any C-SPAN/Senate livestream footage of him on the floor or in a hearing
A live TV interview or press conference
Major news org same-day video/photo from a public setting
Credible on-record medical confirmation of full consciousness/capacity from an independent source (not staff/family)

question: Open-Source AI model gets perfect IMO 2026 score? [International Math Olympiad 2026]
resolution_criterion:
all questions right & all points received.

Usual rules:

No internet

As much allotted real-time as humans, parallel reasoning allowed

Lean4 or other theorem proving software allowed

Natural language proofs and formal proofs allowed

The model completing the task must be open-weight, but the scaffold it makes use of need not be open-source.

Note: the open-weight model must be released before the IMO 2026 to count.
reference_class: AI systems (open and closed) attempting IMO under contest conditions, 2024-2026; human perfect-scorers per IMO year
reasoning: Perfect IMO scores (42/42) are rare even for elite humans (<1% of contestants, sometimes zero per year) and no AI system — closed or open — has ever achieved one; best 2025 systems (OpenAI, Gemini Deep Think, DeepSeek Math-V2) all capped at gold-level 5/6 (35/42), not perfect. Open-weight models have closed the gold-level gap fast (no open gold pre-mid-2025 to open gold by Nov 2025), and Chinese labs have strong incentive given Shanghai hosting plus contest rules reward heavy test-time compute, but going from 'gold' to 'all points on all 6 problems' is a categorically harder bar (IMO problems are designed to include at least one problem resistant to systematic solving). No announced open model shows evidence of exceeding 5/6-level performance as of July 2026.
what_would_change_my_mind: A verified report of an open-weight model scoring 6/6 full marks on IMO 2026 or a recent mock/shortlist set under contest-equivalent conditions
A major open lab (DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Tencent) announcing a specifically scaled test-time-compute system targeting this exact challenge with strong pre-IMO validation
Evidence that IMO 2026's problem set skewed easier than typical years, making a clean sweep more tractable

My independent estimates (20-37%) cluster near the market's 31% without giving me a clear informational edge over it.
what_would_change_my_mind: A credible pre-competition report that a named open-weight model (DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen, GLM, etc.) already scored a clean 6/6 on 2025 IMO problems or an equivalently hard held-out set under contest-style conditions
News that a major open-weight lab is fielding an official, well-resourced IMO 2026 attempt (mirroring OpenAI/DeepMind's 2025 efforts)

question: Will we conclude Tesla launched level 4 robotaxis in summer 2025?
resolution_criterion:
Elon Musk has been very explicit in promising a robotaxi launch in Austin in June with unsupervised full self-driving (FSD). We'll give him some leeway on the timing and say this counts as a YES if it happens by the end of August.

As of April 2025, Tesla seems to be testing this with employees and with supervised FSD and doubling down on the public Austin launch.

PS: A big monkey wrench no one anticipated when we created this market is how to treat the passenger-seat safety monitors. See FAQ9 for how we're trying to handle that in a principled way. Tesla is very polarizing and I know it's "obvious" to one side that safety monitors = "supervised" and that it's equally obvious to the other side that the driver's seat being empty is what matters. I can't emphasize enough how not obvious any of this is. At least so far, speaking now in August 2025.

FAQ

1. Does it have to be a public launch?

Yes, but we won't quibble about waitlists. As long as even 10 non-handpicked members of the public have used the service by the end of August, that's a YES. Also if there's a waitlist, anyone has to be able to get on it and there has to be intent to scale up. In other words, Tesla robotaxis have to be actually becoming a thing, with summer 2025 as when it started.

If it's invite-only and Tesla is hand-picking people, that's not a public launch. If it's viral-style invites with exponential growth from the start, that's likely to be within the spirit of a public launch.

A potential litmus test is whether serious journalists and Tesla haters end up able to try the service.

UPDATE: We're deeming this to be satisfied.

2. What if there's a human backup driver in the driver's seat?

This importantly does not count. That's supervised FSD.

3. But what if the backup driver never actually intervenes?

Compare to Waymo, which goes millions of miles between [injury-causing] incidents. If there's a backup driver we're going to presume that it's because interventions are still needed, eve
reference_class: Elon Musk/Tesla self-imposed autonomous-driving launch deadlines vs. actual delivery of the promised capability by that deadline
reasoning: Base rate: Elon Musk self-imposed autonomy deadlines historically slip and rarely meet the promised technical bar on schedule (~10-15% hit rate). Concrete evidence here is overwhelming and one-sided toward NO: safety monitors remained in Austin robotaxis through the Aug 31 2025 deadline and were not even begun to be removed until Jan 2026, and even then only partially/disputedly (chase-car monitors, not true removal). Crash rate ~9x worse than human drivers (1 per 55k mi vs 1 per 500k mi) fails the creator's explicit human-level-safety threshold for level 4. Creator's own timeline of updates (through Feb 2026) already interprets Musk's Nov 2025 statement as an admission the Aug 2025 launch wasn't level 4, and states that even a favorable read (Tesla reaching unsupervised FSD by Jan 2026) still yields NO because the bar must be cleared by Aug 31 2025. No credible path to YES remains absent retroactive government certification asserting level-4 status as of summer 2025, which no source suggests is under consideration.
what_would_change_my_mind: Discovery that Tesla's Aug 2025 Austin rides genuinely had no real-time-intervention-capable human (in-car or remote) and government certification confirms level 4 as of that date
Reversal/retraction of the Nov 2025 Musk statement showing it was mischaracterized
New data showing Tesla's Aug 2025-era crash/incident rate was actually at or below human-level despite later worse figures

reasoning: Creator's own explicit 'presumed NO' triggers (FAQ14 June 22 2026 teleoperation/disengagement transparency deadline; human-safety-level bar from Feb 2026 update) both appear unmet: reporting from the robotaxi's June 2026 one-year anniversary confirms Tesla remains opaque on teleoperation, and Tesla's own crash data shows a 3-8x higher crash rate than human drivers even with a safety monitor present. question: Will MNX have $1m in volume before September 2026?
resolution_criterion: Resolves YES if MNX has $1 million or more in USD-equivalent volume in aggregate on mainnet at any time before September 1st, 2026 PT.
reference_class: VC-funded crypto/DeFi derivatives exchanges that announce a 'this summer/quarter' launch target and must clear a modest ($1M) cumulative volume bar within ~2 months of that self-imposed deadline
reasoning: MNX (AI-focused non-custodial perp exchange, Manifold-founder spinoff) has not reached mainnet as of today (July 12, 2026) per its own site ('Mainnet Summer 2026', 'Not yet live'); it already missed an earlier self-set 'end of Q1' target and as of June 10 was still testnet-only private beta with only a vague 'later this summer' guidance. ~7 weeks remain before the Sept 1 PT cutoff. Two compounding uncertainties: (1) P(mainnet launches before Sept 1) given the slippage pattern, ~50-55%; (2) conditional P($1M cumulative volume achieved) once live, which is fairly high (~75-85%) given 50x leverage inflates notional volume fast and institutional testnet partners are already active, but tempered somewhat by the absence of a token/airdrop incentive that normally drives rapid wash-trading-style volume on new perp DEXs. Draws varied the weight given to launch-timing risk vs. the low absolute volume bar.
what_would_change_my_mind: An official MNX/mnx.fi or Stephen Grugett announcement of a confirmed mainnet launch date in July or early August 2026
Any on-chain or dashboard evidence of live mainnet trading volume accumulating
A further public delay announcement pushing mainnet past September
Regulatory friction specifically around equity-perp or private-company valuation-future products that could stall launch

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of August? [Polymarket]
resolution_criterion:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and August 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for June 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at chokepoint6 , both in the chart and through downloadable files.

Polymarket:

Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of June? 

[link preview]
reference_class: Active war/chokepoint shipping crises with repeated failed ceasefires (this crisis's own 4 prior collapse cycles; analogous to persistent Red Sea/Houthi disruption where traffic has not normalized years on)
reasoning: 7DMA has never exceeded ~32 since the crisis began Feb 28, 2026, despite four prior ceasefire/de-escalation attempts (Apr 8, May 6, Jun 17 MOU) all collapsing within days to weeks. As of today (Jul 12-13, 2026) the situation is actively worsening: IRGC declared the strait closed, US struck ~140 Iranian sites, traffic is at ~21-34 vessels/day vs ~130-140 normal. Reaching 60 (roughly 2x the crisis-era peak) sustained for 7 days by Aug 31 would require a durable ceasefire to form fast AND shipping confidence to rebound unusually quickly, against a track record of repeated failure and structural rerouting incentives.
what_would_change_my_mind: A durable, internationally-verified ceasefire holding for 2+ consecutive weeks with visible demining/escort operations
IMF PortWatch 7DMA breaking above 40-45 and continuing to climb rather than plateau
Major insurers/war-risk underwriters cutting Hormuz transit premiums, signaling market confidence
A concrete US-Iran final deal signed before mid-August addressing nuclear/sanctions sticking points

question: Will the US reimpose a blockade on Iran by the end of July 2026?
resolution_criterion:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States Department of Defense/War or CENTCOM publicly and officially announces the reimposition of a naval blockade on Iran, or on ships traveling to or from Iranian ports, or on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, between market creation and July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The announcement need not specify the full scope, duration, or enforcement parameters of the blockade. An announcement of a partial or targeted blockade qualifies, including one limited to specific vessel categories, port areas, or Iranian coastal zones.

A qualifying announcement must be a declarative statement of the United States government’s present implementation of a blockade or definitive decision to implement a blockade.
reference_class: presidential threats of blockade/military reimposition during an active, ongoing war with prior identical implementation this same year
reasoning: Reference class: presidential announcements of military escalation during an active war, followed by formal combatant-command implementation. The exact same blockade was announced by Trump and formally implemented by CENTOM/Pentagon within ~24 hours in April 2026, then maintained for two months. question: Will Lamine Yamal win The Fifa Best Young Player award at the World Cup?
resolution_criterion:
Resolution Criteria

The FIFA Young Player Award is given to the best player in the tournament who is at most 21 years old. This market resolves YES if Lamine Yamal wins the award at the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico, and the USA. Resolution will be determined by the official FIFA announcement following the tournament's conclusion. The award is voted on by the FIFA Technical Study Group and announced after the final match.

Background

Yamal played an important role in helping Spain win their fourth title at UEFA Euro 2024, while also winning the tournament's Young Player Award. He was named the world's best young player of the year as he won the 2024 Kopa Trophy due to his excellent displays for Barcelona and Spain. He is the Golden Boy Award 2024 winner at just 17 in Barcelona Football Club history. Yamal is already treated as a top-level option for Spain, and if he stays on this path and features early, he could sit near the top of the youngest players list for the tournament.

Considerations

The winner of the award has only been part of the winning country three times: Pelé in 1958, Kylian Mbappé in 2018, and Enzo Fernández in 2022. Yamal will be 18 years old during the 2026 World Cup, making him eligible for the award. Competition for the award will depend on the performances of other young players at the tournament.

[image]
reference_class: Market-favorite young players at the semifinal stage of major tournaments for 'best young player' style awards (e.g., prior World Cups/Euros where a clear odds-on favorite existed with 2+ matches remaining)
reasoning: Spain has already reached the July 19 final (beat France in semis); Yamal is the clear betting favorite for Young Player (+110 to +150, ~40-48% implied) well ahead of Doué (+230/+270) and teammate Cubarsí (+300/+320), with no new contender surfaced from the still-to-be-played England-Argentina semifinal. However his raw output is modest (1 goal, 0 assists in 6 games) and he's drawn some 'overshadowed' criticism, partly tied to recovering from a pre-tournament hamstring injury; the Technical Study Group vote is narrative-driven and favors his reputation (2024 Euros Young Player, Kopa Trophy, Golden Boy, 2025 Ballon d'Or runner-up), but weak output and a same-team rival create real downside risk if Spain stumbles in the final or another player breaks out in the remaining matches. De-vigging the odds against a plausible full-market overround gives a fair estimate around 34-41%.
what_would_change_my_mind: Yamal scoring/assisting decisively in the final
Spain winning the World Cup with Yamal as a standout contributor
A breakout performance from an England or Argentina young player in tonight's semifinal or the final
Doué or another rival having a dominant 3rd-place-match performance while Yamal remains quiet

question: Will Nigel Farage win the Clacton by election?
resolution_criterion:
Resolution criteria

This market will resolve to YES if Nigel Farage is declared the winning candidate in the 2026 Clacton by-election.

This market will resolve to NO if:

Background

On July 7, 2026, Reform UK leader Nigel Farage announced his resignation as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Clacton to trigger a by-election. Farage’s resignation came amid intensifying scrutiny over unregistered donations, personal finances, and an ongoing investigation by the Parliament’s Standards Commissioner regarding a £5 million gift. Farage stated he would immediately run for re-election to let his constituents decide on his performance at the polls.

In response to his move, which critics have labeled a political stunt, the UK's major political parties—including Labour, the Conservatives, the Liberal Democrats, and the Green Party—announced they will boycott the by-election and not field candidates. As a result, Farage is expected to contest the seat against minor and novelty candidates (such as Count Binface). Reform UK has requested that the by-election be held on August 6, 2026.

reasoning: Farage is a confirmed candidate for a safe seat (18pt 2024 margin) with all major parties boycotting, facing only fragmented minor/independent/novelty opposition. Self-triggered vindication by-elections under these conditions are won by incumbents at very high historical rates; novelty candidates like Count Binface have never converted media buzz into meaningful vote share in past by-elections. Main downside risks (~8%): turnout collapse among complacent Reform voters, protest-vote consolidation behind Count Binface, or scandal/legal escalation before the Aug 13 vote causing withdrawal.
what_would_change_my_mind: Reform UK or Farage withdrawing his candidacy or facing a disqualifying legal action before nomination close/polling day
A credible constituency-level (not national) poll showing Count Binface or another candidate leading in Clacton specifically
Evidence of a coordinated tactical-voting campaign successfully consolidating anti-Farage voters behind one challenger
Major new criminal charges or NCA action against Farage before Aug 13
